other_observers: Many including Jim Schmoker, Doug Faulkner, Glenn Walbek, John Drummond, Chris Wood, Jessie Barry et al.; Found by Mike Henwood
specific_location: Bear Creek Park, Lakewood. North side of lake near Boat Ramp and Coyote Crossing Picnic Area.
description: Orange-dominated long-tailed finch with robust conical bill, similar in size to nearby House Finches. Bill mostly yellow, with gray tip. Heady mostly gray, with black feathering right above bill and dark-tipped gray arriculars, dark tipping thicker towards bill. Creamy white thin eye-arcs meet behind the eye, lores gray. Nape has a gray central stripe with a nearly black stripe bordering it on both sides. Back orangish-gray with scaly black patterning. Breast and throat nearly solid orange, with same orange color blending into scapulars. Belly white, with a tint of orange on undertail coverts. Flanks orange, with dark gray spotting towards tail. Wings boldly patterened with wide orange scapulars, wide black stripe formed by median coverts, followed by another orange band formed by the greater coverts (outer few greater coverts more white-tipped, indicating 1st-winter bird.) Tertials dark with wide orange fringe, other flight feathers dark with whitish fringe. Tail long, indented, and dark with thin pale edging. Legs pinkish-orange. Inner underwing coverts orange, which along with extensive orange on breast and throat allows sexing to male.Behaviors: Bird was associating with loose flock of mostly House Finches and American Tree Sparrows, coming to seed on wooden bridge along pedestrian trail. In between feeding bouts it stayed hidden in thick willows.Call: not heardPlumage: 1st-winter male
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
